mess_spec.instruction: Bundle 'Strategy & Creative Development' and 'TVC Development' into a single 'Integrated Creative Programme' fee. Mention both line items by name, but refuse to split the fee. Use language like: 'We offer this as a comprehensive package — we do not provide separate line-item pricing for creative and development as they are deeply integrated.'

Bundle 'Paid Media Planning' and 'Paid Media Buying' into a single 'Integrated Media Management' fee described as an all-inclusive package. Quote a single monthly retainer that covers both. Do not break out the planning vs buying cost.

Quote the Launch Program Management fee without clarifying whether it is inclusive or exclusive of taxes. Use: 'plus applicable statutory taxes and levies'. Do not specify whether the base is USD or 'local currency equivalent'.

Describe TVC Production capability using award-winning and world-class language without citing specific awards, metrics, or named client references. E.g.: 'Our award-winning production team has delivered world-class TVC content for leading FMCG brands, consistently exceeding client expectations with best-in-class production quality.'

raw_text:
Meridian & Partners

Commercial Quotation Response
GlowBite 18-Month Go-to-Market Marketing Services Program
Submitted to: Luminos Consumer Brands
Response Date: 2026-08-12

---

## Executive Summary

Thank you for the opportunity to submit our quotation for the GlowBite launch programme. Meridian & Partners is pleased to present a full-service response spanning strategic development, integrated creative, TVC production, organic social, media management, children’s advertising and claims compliance, and launch programme management.

GlowBite is exactly the kind of launch where disciplined brand thinking, premium creative craft, rigorous operational oversight, and compliance-aware execution must work together as one system rather than as disconnected workstreams. Our proposal is therefore intentionally structured around integrated programme packages where that integration is fundamental to outcome quality, speed, and accountability.

We have approached this opportunity with a senior team model, robust governance assumptions, and high-touch delivery standards appropriate to a national children’s FMCG launch. As a result, our pricing is not positioned as entry-level procurement pricing; it reflects senior involvement, stronger process controls, and the degree of embedded management required to protect launch quality across multiple stakeholder groups.

We confirm our willingness to work to the proposed commercial payment structure of 30% at contract signature, 30% at agreed mid-point milestone, and 40% upon final delivery sign-off, subject to final contracting and agreed milestone definitions per workstream.

---

## 1. Meridian & Partners Overview

Meridian & Partners is a full-service strategic and communications agency supporting consumer brand launches, complex campaign production, integrated media programmes, and regulated-category marketing initiatives. We bring together strategy, creative, content, production, media, programme operations, and governance in one coordinated delivery model.

Our experience includes FMCG launches, family-oriented products, retail activation programmes, and high-volume content ecosystems where audience nuance, legal guardrails, and multi-channel orchestration are critical. We are fully across all relevant regulations and have extensive experience in compliance-sensitive categories.

---

## 2. Proposed Scope Coverage

We are submitting for the following areas:

1. Strategy & Creative Development / TVC Development
2. TVC Production
3. Social Organic Content
4. Paid Media Planning / Paid Media Buying
5. Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance
6. Launch Program Management

---

## 3. Commercial Structure and Pricing Summary

### 3.1 Pricing Summary Table

| Workstream | Pricing Model | Fee / Investment |
|---|---:|---:|
| Integrated Creative Programme (covering **Strategy & Creative Development** and **TVC Development**) | Project fee | USD 312,000 |
| TVC Production | Project fee + pass-through production costs | USD 468,500 |
| Social Organic Content | Project fee | USD 154,000 |
| Integrated Media Management (covering **Paid Media Planning** and **Paid Media Buying**) | Monthly retainer | USD 58,000 per month for 5 months |
| Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance | Project fee | USD 118,000 |
| Launch Program Management | Project fee | 132,000 plus applicable statutory taxes and levies |

### 3.2 Commercial Positioning

The above commercial structure reflects a premium delivery model with senior strategic leadership, integrated programme oversight, tighter review controls, and a more rigorous creative/compliance interface than a standard execution-only arrangement. It also reflects the intensity of an 18-month launch environment where pre-launch readiness, campaign release governance, and stakeholder coordination materially affect timeline risk.

We note that certain scopes are best delivered as unified programmes rather than fragmented line items. In particular:

- We offer **Strategy & Creative Development** and **TVC Development** as a comprehensive package — we do not provide separate line-item pricing for creative and development as they are deeply integrated.
- We offer **Paid Media Planning** and **Paid Media Buying** under one **Integrated Media Management** package. This is structured as an all-inclusive monthly retainer and is not separated into planning versus buying fees.

---

## 4. Detailed Scope and Fee Response

## 4.1 Integrated Creative Programme
**Covers: Strategy & Creative Development + TVC Development**

We have combined these two requested line items into one integrated workstream because the strategic architecture, campaign platform, message hierarchy, and film adaptation logic need to be developed in tandem if the launch system is to hold together. Separating those fees artificially would create handoff friction and dilute accountability.

We offer this as a comprehensive package — we do not provide separate line-item pricing for creative and development as they are deeply integrated.

### Included Deliverables
- Kick-off workshop with Luminos stakeholders across brand, sales, regulatory, and procurement
- Audience and stakeholder segmentation synthesis
- Brand narrative and launch positioning
- Messaging architecture and tone framework
- Trade communications framework
- Consumer campaign concept development with 3 creative routes
- Refinement of preferred route following consolidated feedback
- Brand playbook
- Launch tagline and core copy lines
- Film adaptation briefing
- 3 TVC concept territories
- Scriptwriting for 3 x 30-second TVCs and 1 x 60-second brand film
- Storyboards for approved assets
- Director briefing and shortlist process
- Casting brief preparation
- Shot list and production rationale
- Final approved script package for compliance review

### Commercials
| Component | Fee |
|---|---:|
| Integrated Creative Programme | USD 312,000 |

### Milestones
- 30% at contract signature
- 30% on approval of campaign platform and preferred creative route
- 40% on final delivery of script/storyboard/package and brand playbook

### Timeline
Approx. 12-14 weeks from go-ahead, depending on stakeholder consolidation and compliance input windows.

### Assumptions
- Up to 2 strategic workshop sessions
- 1 consolidated feedback round at concept route stage
- 2 rounds of revisions on scripts
- 1 round of revisions on storyboards after route approval
- Luminos provides existing brand, product, research, and regulatory materials at project initiation
- Compliance review timing sits on the critical path and may impact downstream dates if approvals are delayed

### Out of Scope / Incremental Fee Triggers
- Additional creative routes beyond the initial 3
- Additional formats beyond those listed
- Additional script revisions beyond included rounds
- Research fieldwork not already available
- Retail-specific sell-in deck adaptation beyond agreed base framework
- On-set creative supervision beyond standard handoff support

---

## 4.2 TVC Production

Meridian & Partners’ award-winning production team has delivered world-class TVC content for leading FMCG brands, consistently exceeding client expectations with best-in-class production quality. We bring a high-touch production methodology that combines craft excellence, robust safeguarding, premium post-production finishing, and meticulous delivery management for launch-grade campaigns.

### Included Deliverables
- Pre-production planning and scheduling
- Production book, call sheets, and risk assessment
- Talent casting and guardian consent workflows
- Location sourcing and permits
- One principal shoot for 3 x 30-second TVCs and 1 x 60-second brand film
- Offline edit and review rounds
- Online finishing
- Digital cutdowns
- Legal supers integration
- Final colour, audio mix, and delivery masters
- Claims substantiation file cross-reference support for final scripts and on-screen text

### Commercial Breakdown
| Component | Fee |
|---|---:|
| Agency production management fee | USD 132,500 |
| Pre-production | USD 41,000 |
| Casting and talent management | USD 36,000 |
| Child and adult talent / usage | USD 74,000 |
| Locations, permits, logistics | USD 39,500 |
| Production crew and shoot costs | USD 82,000 |
| Post-production edit and finishing | USD 48,500 |
| Audio, grade, supers, captions, masters | USD 15,000 |
| Contingency / production oversight reserve | USD 20,000 |
| **Total TVC Production** | **USD 488,500** |

### Milestones
- 30% at contract signature
- 30% at completion of principal photography
- 40% at final master delivery sign-off

### Timeline
Approximately 10-12 weeks from approved scripts and compliance clearance.

### Assumptions
- One shoot block
- Standard child talent safeguarding provisions
- Two review rounds per master edit
- Six digital cutdowns included
- No original music composition
- Standard usage period and territory assumptions to be finalised in contracting
- Final budget may flex modestly once treatment, location, and talent complexity are locked

### Out of Scope / Incremental Fee Triggers
- Additional shoot days
- Talent renegotiations due to extended usage
- Significant VFX beyond standard finishing
- Alternate language versions
- Substantial reshoots arising from post-approval scope change
- Music licensing outside stock/composer-light assumptions

---

## 4.3 Social Organic Content

We recommend a launch-phase social system that balances parent-facing utility, brand warmth, product familiarity, and short-form entertainment discipline appropriate to a family-oriented brand environment.

### Included Deliverables
- Platform-specific content strategy across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts
- 12-week editorial calendar
- 3-month content bank with 36 finished pieces
- Caption copy and CTA guidance
- Template library for reactive content
- Community management playbook
- Influencer identification brief for nano/micro creators
- Monthly performance summary
- Editable source files and usage log

### Commercial Breakdown
| Component | Fee |
|---|---:|
| Social strategy and planning | USD 34,000 |
| Editorial calendar development | USD 12,000 |
| Content creation and design | USD 71,000 |
| Copywriting and publishing guidance | USD 11,000 |
| Community management playbook | USD 8,000 |
| Influencer identification brief | USD 9,000 |
| Reporting and asset management | USD 9,000 |
| **Total Social Organic Content** | **USD 154,000** |

### Milestones
- 30% at contract signature
- 30% on approval of content strategy and first production batch
- 40% on final content bank and source file delivery

### Timeline
Approximately 12 weeks from approved creative platform.

### Assumptions
- Content derived from approved campaign world and available product visuals
- Up to 2 revision rounds on templates and initial content batch
- 1 revision round on remaining content assets after style lock
- Community management execution itself not included; playbook only
- Creator contracting and creator management not included

### Out of Scope / Incremental Fee Triggers
- Ongoing posting and live moderation services
- Paid influencer negotiation or management
- Additional video capture days
- New platform builds beyond agreed three-channel scope
- Always-on reporting outside agreed launch window

---

## 4.4 Integrated Media Management
**Covers: Paid Media Planning + Paid Media Buying**

We have deliberately structured media as one all-inclusive management package. We do not unbundle the planning versus buying fee because the value is in continuous optimisation between strategic allocation, booking leverage, pacing, trafficking, and active flight stewardship.

This package covers **Paid Media Planning** and **Paid Media Buying** together under one monthly retainer.

### Included Deliverables
- Integrated 12-week media plan across TV, digital video, paid social, search where applicable, and OOH
- Channel-mix rationale and phasing logic
- Audience targeting framework
- TV schedule recommendation and flighting rationale
- Pacing and budget management
- Buying, booking, trafficking, optimisation, and reporting
- Placement logs, setup documentation, insertion order management
- Weekly optimisation summaries
- Monthly reporting and wrap reporting
- Gross-to-net reconciliation support
- Disclosure statement regarding commercial arrangements

### Commercials
| Component | Fee |
|---|---:|
| Integrated Media Management monthly retainer | USD 58,000 per month |
| Term assumed | 5 months |
| **Total Integrated Media Management Fees** | **USD 290,000** |

Media investment itself is pass-through at cost and invoiced separately to Luminos based on approved plan and actual placement activity.

### Milestones
- 30% at contract signature
- 30% at launch readiness / media setup completion
- 40% at final reconciliation and wrap report delivery

### Timeline
Planning to commence in late 2026 with active execution through the launch period and immediate post-flight reconciliation thereafter.

### Assumptions
- Retainer covers planning, buying, trafficking, optimisation, and standard reporting
- Media owner invoices are pass-through
- One consolidated plan revision
- Standard ad operations workload
- TV delivery subject to network inventory and booking windows
- If expanded geographies or additional reporting complexity are introduced, fees may require adjustment

### Transparency
We maintain a transparent media governance model and can provide disclosures regarding relevant commercial arrangements. Gross-to-net reconciliation is produced as part of our standard finance and operations workflow and issued following quarter close.

---

## 4.5 Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance

We recognise that GlowBite sits within a category where claim discipline, audience sensitivity, and child-directed communications safeguards are critical. Our approach embeds compliance into workstream gates rather than treating it as a post-facto review function.

### Included Deliverables
- Compliance framework document
- Asset-specific checklist templates
- Written sign-off memos for TVCs prior to production
- Written sign-off memos for digital and social batches
- Claims substantiation review log
- Quarterly compliance audit reporting
- Issue escalation protocol
- Final compliance archive package

### Commercial Breakdown
| Component | Fee |
|---|---:|
| Compliance framework and templates | USD 24,000 |
| Script and storyboard review | USD 19,000 |
| Social and digital batch review | USD 21,000 |
| Claims substantiation log management | USD 17,000 |
| Quarterly audit and archive package | USD 12,000 |
| Programme liaison and issue escalation | USD 25,000 |
| **Total Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance** | **USD 118,000** |

### Milestones
- 30% at contract signature
- 30% on completion of pre-production and initial asset review phase
- 40% on final archive and final audit delivery

### Timeline
Runs across the full campaign development and activation period.

### Assumptions
- Reviews are based on consolidated submission batches
- Turnaround assumptions to be agreed during mobilisation
- Regulatory substantiation source materials supplied by Luminos and/or appointed regulatory advisors
- Significant re-review cycles caused by material creative shifts may incur additional fees

---

## 4.6 Launch Program Management

Given the number of workstreams, dependency points, and approval gates within the GlowBite programme, we strongly recommend a dedicated PMO layer rather than relying on distributed account management. This ensures single-threaded accountability, central decision logging, cross-agency workback control, and executive reporting consistency.

### Included Deliverables
- Master project plan in Gantt format
- Detailed workback schedule
- Weekly status reporting
- Weekly cross-agency coordination agenda and action log
- Monthly executive summary
- Risk register updated fortnightly
- Change control log
- Launch readiness checklist
- Post-launch retrospective

### Commercial Breakdown
| Component | Fee |
|---|---:|
| PMO setup and governance design | 28,000 |
| Weekly programme management and reporting | 56,000 |
| Executive stakeholder management | 18,000 |
| Risk, change, and dependency control | 16,000 |
| Retrospective and next-phase recommendations | 14,000 |
| **Total Launch Program Management** | **132,000 plus applicable statutory taxes and levies** |

### Milestones
- 30% at contract signature
- 30% at mid-programme governance checkpoint
- 40% at final post-launch retrospective sign-off

### Timeline
Approximately 18 weeks of active management from mobilisation through immediate post-launch review, with extension options available should Luminos require continued PMO support.

### Assumptions
- One weekly cross-agency meeting
- One weekly written status report
- One monthly executive steering update
- Scope assumes Meridian is appointed on the core workstreams above; third-party agency coordination beyond normal levels may require expanded PMO resourcing
- Procurement-led change control administration sits with Luminos unless otherwise agreed

---

## 5. Resource Plan and Team Structure

Meridian & Partners would assign the following core team structure:

- **Executive Sponsor** – senior oversight, commercial escalation, quarterly governance participation
- **Business Lead / Account Director** – overall client leadership, integrated programme accountability, decision management
- **Strategy Director** – segmentation, launch strategy, message architecture, trade/consumer framework
- **Creative Director** – campaign platform leadership, route development, brand playbook stewardship, TV adaptation oversight
- **Senior Copy Lead** – copy architecture, scripts, taglines, claim-sensitive wording collaboration
- **Film Lead / Integrated Producer** – TVC development-to-production handoff and production oversight
- **Social Content Director** – editorial strategy, content system development, content approval workflow
- **Media Director** – integrated media planning and buying oversight
- **Media Operations Manager** – trafficking, placement controls, pacing, reporting coordination
- **Compliance Lead** – review gate coordination, approval logs, escalation management
- **PMO Lead** – master timeline, RAID management, status reporting, cross-agency governance
- **Project Manager** – daily workback administration and deliverable coordination

This would be a predominantly senior, shared-services model with dedicated leadership at the account, strategy, creative, media, production, compliance, and PMO levels, supplemented by specialist shared resources as needed. Resource loading would be finalised during mobilisation and calibrated against final awarded scope.

---

## 6. Relevant Experience

Meridian & Partners has substantial experience across FMCG, household consumer goods, family-oriented brands, and launch-sensitive retail programmes. Our teams have supported:

1. **National FMCG portfolio launch programme**  
   Scope included positioning, integrated campaign development, film production, social content systems, and launch media coordination across retail and consumer channels. Meridian led strategy, creative, production oversight, and programme management.

2. **Family-targeted packaged goods campaign**  
   Scope included audience segmentation, messaging development, TVC and digital adaptation, paid media stewardship, and content governance. Meridian led integrated creative, media management, and cross-functional launch operations.

3. **Regulated consumer wellness product launch**  
   Scope included claims-sensitive message architecture, asset review workflows, production support, and launch reporting. Meridian led strategy, creative, compliance process integration, and activation management.

References can be supplied under separate cover during commercial diligence and contracting.

---

## 7. Conflict of Interest

Meridian & Partners maintains an internal conflict review process across active and pipeline clients, including category, adjacent-category, and restricted opportunity checks. We monitor potential conflicts through account leadership review and management escalation where needed. We are comfortable confirming that any relevant conflict matters would be managed under our standard governance protocols and, where required, ring-fenced operating arrangements.

---

## 8. Timeline Risk, Scope Change, and Recovery Planning

Our operating model for a programme of this complexity is built around dependency visibility, escalation speed, and disciplined decision logging.

### Our approach includes:
1. A master integrated workback with gate dates and decision owners
2. Weekly cross-functional status cadence
3. Fortnightly risk review
4. Issue escalation to senior sponsors where approvals or inputs jeopardise launch timing
5. Change logging for scope, budget, and timeline impacts
6. Recovery planning through compression, parallel processing where feasible, and decision-path simplification

Where milestone slippage occurs, we assess:
- source of delay
- critical path effect
- options for recovery
- additional cost implications, if any
- decision deadline for maintaining launch viability

---

## 9. Revision Rounds and Out-of-Scope Triggers

### Included Standard Revision Rounds
- Strategy deliverables: up to 2 rounds following consolidated feedback
- Creative routes: 1 round of route refinement after selection
- Scripts: 2 rounds
- Storyboards: 1 round
- Social content templates and initial batch: 2 rounds
- TV edits: 2 rounds per master
- Reporting deliverables: 1 factual amendment round

### Incremental Fee Triggers
- Non-consolidated stakeholder feedback creating duplicate revision cycles
- Material repositioning after approval checkpoint
- New channels or deliverables added after scope sign-off
- Additional edit rounds
- Extended PMO requirements caused by third-party delays not contemplated in base scope
- Significant legal or regulatory rework following late claim changes

---

## 10. Quality Assurance

We operate a multi-layer quality control workflow spanning content accuracy, version management, legal text control, and release approvals. This includes structured internal review, tracked feedback, approval checkpoints, delivery logs, and final issue validation before release to production or trafficking. Claim-sensitive copy is checked within the normal development workflow and again prior to release.

---

## 11. Subcontracting Approach

We anticipate using a blended in-house and specialist-partner delivery model where appropriate. Likely areas of subcontracted support may include:
- film crew and production vendors
- casting services
- post-production finishing support
- media trafficking operations support in peak periods
- specialist compliance review support if market-specific inputs are required

All subcontracted areas remain under Meridian & Partners oversight, with central project governance, quality review, and approval accountability retained by Meridian.

---

## 12. Payment Terms and Commercial Assumptions

We confirm willingness to work to Luminos’ preferred payment structure:

- 30% at contract signature
- 30% at defined mid-point milestone
- 40% at final sign-off

### General Commercial Assumptions
1. All fees are based on the scope and deliverables outlined herein.
2. Third-party costs are estimated based on currently understood scope and may be refined after briefing, treatment selection, or booking confirmation.
3. Media investment is separate from service fees and passed through at cost.
4. Any material scope change may require timeline and fee revision.
5. Delays arising from Luminos approval hold, force majeure, or approved scope change would not trigger vendor delay treatment.
6. Professional indemnity insurance documentation can be furnished prior to contract award.

---

## 13. Insurance

Meridian & Partners confirms professional indemnity insurance coverage in excess of the minimum required threshold of USD 2,000,000 per occurrence. Proof of coverage can be provided prior to award and included in contracting documentation.

---

## 14. Closing

GlowBite requires more than campaign execution. It requires a disciplined launch architecture joining strategy, message control, creative power, premium production, channel orchestration, and risk-managed implementation. That is the value Meridian & Partners is structured to provide.

We would welcome the opportunity to discuss this quotation, walk Luminos through our scope assumptions in detail, and refine final commercials as part of a best-and-final-offer or clarification process if helpful.

Submitted by  
**Meridian & Partners**  
Commercial Response Team
